title: Budget and Structured Compaction

classes.name: TokenEstimator
classes.purpose: Pluggable token counter; v0.04 ships Char4TokenEstimator default

classes.name: Char4TokenEstimator
classes.purpose: Conservative fallback: 1 token per 4 UTF-8 bytes; safe for CJK and Latin

classes.name: CompactionPolicy
classes.purpose: Stateless policy that merges evicted observations into summary items

classes.name: CompactionOutcome
classes.purpose: Result of one compaction pass; builder consumes final_selected

design_decisions.rationale: SOP §8.2 allows compressing old Observations; §9.2 forbids rewriting hypothesis as fact. By restricting merge input to observations, the policy structurally prevents forbidden promotions.
A summary is a derived observation, not a verified fact. Promoting it would violate §9.2.
SOP §4.1 requires source traceability. The comma-joined list lets any consumer trace back to originals.
Summaries are produced by deterministic code, not by an LLM. v0.04 does NOT use model-generated summaries.

design_decisions.decision: Compaction hash excludes summary item_id (random uuid), includes only content hash
design_decisions.rationale: D6 invariant: two compactions of the same eligible set must produce the same hash. Random uuid would break determinism.

design_decisions.decision: Post-compaction budget check: only raise ContextBudgetExhausted if PROTECTED items exceed usable_input
design_decisions.rationale: SOP §8.3 clause 4 says 'still over budget → context_budget_exhausted, do not silently truncate hard constraints'. This means protected items must not be dropped. If only summaries push total over usable, we accept the outcome — dropping a summary would lose provenance.

design_decisions.decision: `_is_protected` duplicated in compaction.py (not imported from builder.py)
design_decisions.rationale: Avoids circular import (builder imports CompactionPolicy lazily, compaction would import _is_protected eagerly). The two copies MUST stay in sync.
Allows tests to inject custom policies. Default None → lazy-instantiate CompactionPolicy on first use.

coverage.scenario: Deterministic eviction order (observation before constraint, priority)
Structured compaction (merges evicted, required fields, constraint retention)
Forbidden behaviors (hypothesis, evidence, constraint, protected overflow)
CompactionPolicy direct unit tests (no-eviction, scope, estimator, chunking)

forbidden_behaviors_enforced: hypothesis never promoted to fact (construction: only observations merged)
evidence_ref never fabricated (construction: summary references original item_ids only)
constraint never dropped by compaction (construction: _is_protected restores protected items)
original sources never overwritten (construction: summary is a new item, originals stay in repo)
external_untrusted never promoted to instruction (construction: summary trust_level = trusted_local, scope = shared)
non_blocking_debts: CompactionPolicy._build_summary_item uses a simple content snippet (first 80 chars per item). v0.04.1 may add LLM-based summarization via ModelAdapter.
Large file content (tier 1 in §8.3) is not specially handled in compaction — it's treated as a regular evictable item. v0.04.1 may add source_ref-based re-read eligibility.
Tool output trimming (D3) relies on first-pass eviction; no dedicated `max_tool_output_tokens` enforcement in compaction yet.
